redGreenRefactorEvidence: Red: tests/integration/test_restore_drill.py (pass + corrupted dump fail) written first. Green: restore-drill.sh + helper CLI extensions. Refactor: reused safe_alembic_helpers row_counts/verify_token_decryption with --url instead of duplicating logic.
implementationSummary: Adds VPS-local weekly restore drill: resolve latest juli-pre-migrate dump once, pg_restore into scratch DB on same Postgres instance, verify protected-table row counts and token decrypt via safe_alembic_helpers, drop scratch DB with trap cleanup, log RESTORE DRILL PASS/FAIL for journalctl. Systemd timer/service pair mirrors juli-secrets-refresh pattern.
assumptions: Pre-migration dumps remain in ~/backups on VPS per safe-alembic-upgrade.sh; no off-box backup transport (ADR-027).
Scratch DB CREATE/DROP uses postgres maintenance database on same instance as DATABASE_DIRECT_URL/DATABASE_URL.
risks: Restore drill adds VPS disk/CPU load on a weekly schedule — runs Sunday ~03:00 with randomized delay.
Backup rotation race: drill resolves backup filename once at start; concurrent rotation during long pg_restore could still delete file if drill started before rotation picked a different latest file.
Failed drill may indicate scratch-DB/disk issues rather than unusable production backups — runbook documents triage.
